summary: Build (tsc --noEmit) passes clean, all 115 tests pass. The implementation correctly replaces threadId→channelId across 44 files in types, repos, services, API routes, commands, handlers, orchestrator, and web frontend. New ChannelService with channel-repo, claude-session-repo, sync-cursor-repo is well structured. DB column mappings are correct (drive_thread_id DB column maps to goalChannelId in code; channel_id column in tasks table maps correctly). Dual-write to new tables and Discord event sync are properly implemented. Previous audit's critical errors (table name mismatches, SQL binding mismatches) have all been resolved. Remaining issues are comment/parameter naming inconsistencies (warning level only).

issues.description: notify() method parameter still named 'threadId' (and internal refs at lines 2825, 2832, 2837). Should be renamed to 'channelId' for consistency with the rename.
HandleReplanDeps.notify callback parameter still named 'threadId'. Should be 'channelId' for consistency.
Lambda parameter (threadId, message, type, options) => this.notify(threadId, ...) should use channelId for consistency.
GoalDetail.drive_channel_id doesn't match actual DB column name 'drive_thread_id'. Type-only issue (no runtime impact since GoalDetail is not used in route handlers), but could confuse API consumers.
JSDoc comments still reference ':threadId' URL parameter (lines 6-10, 171, 197, 268, 322, 350, 416). Routes were actually renamed to ':channelId' in server.ts.
JSDoc comment still references ':threadId' URL parameter. Should be ':channelId'.
JSDoc comments (lines 2-5) still reference ':threadId' URL parameter. Should be ':channelId'.
JSDoc comment still references ':threadId' URL parameter. Should be ':channelId'.
Fire-and-forget async in setSessionClaudeId(): claudeSessionRepo.getActiveByChannel().then() lacks .catch() error handler (also at line 482 in archiveSession). Potential unhandled promise rejection.
